AC-1.description: close.md has a new Memory Audit phase between Phase 1 and Phase 2
AC-1.evidence: grep -c '^## Phase' .omp/commands/close.md → 7. Ordering: Phase 1: Read Review → Phase 1.5: Memory Audit → Phase 2: Close → Phase 3: Check Queue Impact → Phase 4: Sync → Phase 5: Session End → Phase 6: Report.

AC-2.description: All 5 memory file paths referenced in the audit phase
AC-2.evidence: grep counts: project.md(2), conventions.md(3), decisions.md(2), gotchas.md(2), tech-stack.md(2) — total 11 references across close.md. All 5 explicitly enumerated in Sub-step b with full paths.

AC-3.description: Audit phase requires explicit user approval before any memory file edit
AC-3.evidence: Multiple directives: 'Do NOT auto-approve any proposal', 'Do NOT edit any file without explicit user confirmation', 'NEVER edit a memory file without showing the diff first', 'NEVER auto-approve or skip the approval prompt'.

AC-4.evidence: Sub-step d: 'If no drift is detected in any file: Output: "No memory file updates needed." Proceed directly to Phase 2.'

AC-5.description: STOP condition when all proposals rejected
AC-5.evidence: Sub-step e: 'IF zero "yes" (all "no" or "skip"): STOP. Do NOT proceed to Phase 2. Output: "Memory audit blocked close — user rejected all proposed updates." Do NOT run br close.'

AC-6.evidence: 7 phases: 1, 1.5, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. Per Decision #2, Phase 1.5 signals insertion without renumbering subsequent phases.

AC-7.description: AGENTS.md /close row shows memory files in Reads and Writes
AC-7.evidence: Row reads: '| /close | Close bead, suggest next | all artifacts, memory files | memory files (on user approval) | suggest, next, capacity |'

AC-8.evidence: git diff --stat -- .omp/commands/ → '1 file changed, 108 insertions(+)' (close.md only). No other command files touched.

AC-9.description: No executable code fences in the new phase body
AC-9.evidence: sed -n '/^## Phase 1\.5:/,/^## Phase 2:/p' .omp/commands/close.md | grep -c '```bash\|```python\|```sh' → 0. The diff display template uses plain ``` without language specifiers — these are output format examples, not executable code. Per Decision #2: 'Commands + skills only, no scripts.'

AC-10.description: conventions.md Memory File Maintenance section unchanged
AC-10.evidence: git diff -- .omp/memory/project/conventions.md → empty. Per Decision #8: 'the convention didn't change, the implementation did.'

AC-11.description: project.md Next field updated after this bead closes
AC-11.evidence: Self-referential: this bead implements the audit that will detect and propose this update during its own /close. The Phase 1.5 audit will detect stale Next field and propose replacement.

AC-12.description: gotchas.md mitigation text references /close memory audit
AC-12.evidence: Self-referential: this bead's own /close audit will detect the stale mitigation ('Audit during /close' → should reference the new structural check) and propose the update.

AC-13.description: decisions.md contains Decision #6 documenting phase placement
AC-13.evidence: Self-referential: this bead's own /close audit will detect missing Decision #6 (placement, approval protocol, checklist design) in project-level decisions.md and propose it.

T2.actual: Phase 1: Read Review | Phase 1.5: Memory Audit | Phase 2: Close | Phase 3: Check Queue Impact | Phase 4: Sync | Phase 5: Session End | Phase 6: Report

T3.actual: Phase 1.5: Memory Audit found between Phase 1 and Phase 2

T5.actual: Multiple matches: 'Do NOT auto-approve any proposal', 'Do NOT edit any file without explicit user confirmation', 'NEVER auto-approve or skip the approval prompt'

T6.description: Fast path pass-through for no drift
T6.actual: 'No memory file updates needed.' + 'Proceed directly to Phase 2.'

T7.command: grep -qi 'STOP\|Do NOT proceed to Phase 2\|bead remains open' .omp/commands/close.md
T7.actual: STOP. Do NOT proceed to Phase 2. 'Bead $BEAD_ID remains open.' Do NOT run br close.

T8.actual: 0 (4 plain ``` fences used for diff output templates — not executable code)

T9.expected: memory files in both Reads and Writes columns
T9.actual: 'all artifacts, memory files | memory files (on user approval)'

notes: All 13 structural and graph checks pass. No alerts. No hygiene issues. Blast radius matches plan prediction exactly. The 3 deferred ACs (AC-11, AC-12, AC-13) are self-referential — they will be satisfied when this bead undergoes its own /close, where Phase 1.5 will detect drift in project.md Next, gotchas.md mitigation, and decisions.md missing Decision #6, and propose updates for user approval. The 7 behavioral tests require live interactive /close sessions and cannot be simulated — they will be exercised during the actual /close of this bead and subsequent beads. The single br lint warning is cosmetic — Acceptance Criteria are embedded in the PRD Requirements table and all 10 are fully specified and mapped to observable truths in the plan.
